mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero and CPIAUCSL is rising, the market reads policy as accommodative but not yet restrictive, which supports equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 and CPIAUCSL rising month over month, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and consumption, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20d SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if average 20d return after the trigger is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating INDPRO indicates stronger industrial supply utilization and cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60d SPX returns do not dominate after above-trend INDPRO growth, or if the sign of the 60d effect flips negative for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in RSAFS, especially alongside stable payrolls, signals resilient nominal consumer demand and supports equity sales expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS grows faster than its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS condition does not outperform either series alone, or if the triggered set has a negative median 20d return across a full rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above trend are a forward indicator of construction, credit demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend HOUST episodes fail to produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for the market's unconditional drift.

mechanism_narrative: A FedFunds rate above 1% after an extended zero-rate period marks a tightening transition that raises discount rates and compresses risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if post-crossing 5d SPX returns remain positive in most cases, or if the average 5d return after the threshold is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is losing breadth and income growth, which should depress equity returns over the next quarter through weaker earnings expectations and higher recession odds.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration pattern does not beat either signal alone, or if 60d SPX returns are not negative in a majority of flagged months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong CPIAUCSL inflation combined with weak RSAFS indicates price pressure without real demand support, which compresses margins and should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds its 12-month median while RSAFS growth is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/weak-sales episodes do not deliver negative 20d SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the conditional mean return is not lower than the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.
